{{Short description="Electromagnetic radiation with the highest photon energy}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Gamma ray
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Electromagnetic wave]], [[Electromagnetic spectrum]], [[Photon]], [[Radioactive decay]], [[Nuclear physics]]
| wavelength = <0.01 nm
| frequency = >30 EHz
| energy = >100 keV
}}

A '''gamma ray''' (γ) is a form of high-energy [[electromagnetic radiation]] with the shortest [[wavelength]] and highest [[photon]] energy in the [[electromagnetic spectrum]]. Gamma rays have photon energies above 100 keV, frequencies above 30 EHz, and wavelengths below 10 pm.

== Origin ==
Gamma rays are produced by:
* [[Radioactive decay]] (gamma decay) — de-excitation of an excited [[atomic nucleus]]
* [[Annihilation]] — electron–positron annihilation producing 511 keV photons
* [[Nuclear reaction]]s — fission, fusion, neutron capture
* [[Astroparticle physics|Astrophysical processes]] — [[supernova]]e, [[gamma-ray burst]]s, [[pulsar]]s, [[active galactic nucleus|active galactic nuclei]]
* [[Particle accelerator]]s — [[bremsstrahlung]] from high-energy electrons

== Properties ==
* Penetrating power: can pass through several cm of lead
* [[Ionizing radiation]] — damages biological tissue
* No electric charge — unaffected by electric or magnetic fields
* Travel at the [[speed of light]]

== Gamma decay ==
When an excited nucleus returns to its ground state, it emits the excess energy as a gamma-ray photon:
:<math>^{A}_{Z}\text{X}^* \rightarrow ^{A}_{Z}\text{X} + \gamma</math>

No change in [[atomic number]] or [[mass number]].

== Interaction with matter ==
Gamma rays interact with matter via three primary mechanisms:
# '''[[Photoelectric effect]]''' — dominant at low energies (<0.5 MeV)
# '''[[Compton scattering]]''' — dominant at intermediate energies (0.5–5 MeV)
# '''[[Pair production]]''' — dominant at high energies (>5 MeV), creating electron–positron pairs

== Applications ==
* '''Medical''' — [[radiotherapy]] (cancer treatment), [[gamma knife]] surgery, sterilization
* '''Industrial''' — non-destructive testing, food irradiation
* '''Science''' — [[gamma-ray astronomy]], [[Mössbauer spectroscopy]]
* '''Security''' — cargo and vehicle scanning

== Gamma-ray bursts ===
{{Main|Gamma-ray burst}}
[[Gamma-ray burst]]s are the most energetic electromagnetic events known, releasing in seconds more energy than the Sun will in its entire 10-billion-year lifetime.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Compton scattering]]
* [[Electromagnetic spectrum]]
* [[Electromagnetic wave]]
* [[Radioactive decay]]
* [[X-ray]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Gamma rays| ]]
[[Category:Electromagnetic radiation]]
[[Category:Radioactivity]]
